rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4792/#4527 as the fix, matching the thread's ground truth almost verbatim in code citations. X is slightly more conservative and grounded — it flags potential red herrings in the reporter's own log (NVFP4 patch line, timeout-value change from 300s→600s) and gives a tiered remediation path (upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround) without inventing unverifiable specifics. Y adds plausible-sounding but unsupported details (a specific unit test name 'validating' the fix, a `SharedMemoryConnector.put()` call, a `custom_process_next_stage_input_func` hook) that aren't corroborated by the ground-truth thread and read as fabrication risk, slightly hurting its grounding and trustworthiness.
